steps.title: Substitute and set up the equation
Square both sides and form the quadratic
steps.content: Square both sides: $$45p + 144 = p^2$$ Rearrange to standard form: $$p^2 - 45p - 144 = 0$$

steps.title: Factor and identify the valid solution
steps.content: Find two numbers that multiply to $-144$ and sum to $-45$: $-48$ and $3$. The quadratic factors as $(p - 48)(p + 3) = 0$, giving $p = 48$ or $p = -3$. Since the original equation $\sqrt{45p + 144} = p$ requires $p \geq 0$ (square roots output non-negative values), only $p = 48$ is valid.
